payload.evidence_summary: The title is explicitly a story DLC for Starfield, and its description is additive rather than contradictory: new locations, technologies, weapons, and story combat against a hostile faction. Per DLC guidance, the parent game's stored evidence is the primary basis for classification. Parent text strongly indicates a Bethesda-style single-player sci-fi RPG with player-created character, open-ended planetary/space exploration, faction quests, dialogue-driven roleplaying, ship combat/customization, loot/crafting, and broad quest-based progression. That supports western_narrative_rpg as the best fit, with open_world_action_adventure as a secondary influence.

Stored DB evidence is too thin for a reliable Similar Games Taxonomy V2 classification. There is no description or gameplay corpus text from Steam, OpenCritic, or Metacritic. The only available signals are broad labels: puzzle, strategy, roguelike, and single-player. Those tags are insufficient to determine a supported archetype from the allowed list or populate a gameplay fingerprint without guessing.
payload.rejection_reason: Insufficient gameplay evidence; only broad genre/theme labels are present and no descriptive text supports a specific taxonomy archetype.

payload.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently portray a single-player fantasy action RPG built around real-time slashing/dodging combat, helping townspeople, exploring the area around Zoz, and progressing through character bonds and quests. Farming/restaurant/cooking systems appear meaningful but supportive rather than the primary gameplay identity, so the strongest fit is an open-world-fantasy-action-RPG-style profile with life-sim overlap. Exact camera/presentation and full world scale are not explicit, so those fields are left blank.

Stored evidence consistently describes a short, low-stress single-player fishing experience with branching narrative decisions, handcrafted art, and an immersive reflective tone. However, the available taxonomy archetype list does not include a clear fishing/narrative-exploration fit, and the evidence does not strongly support any listed archetype such as life_sim or sports_sim. There is also insufficient evidence for horror, management, action, or other supported gameplay identities. Because the gameplay identity appears real but does not map cleanly and confidently to the permitted archetypes, rejection is safer than forcing a weak fit.
payload.rejection_reason: Evidence indicates a narrative fishing sim, but none of the allowed Similar Games Taxonomy V2 archetypes are supported strongly enough by the stored DB evidence.

payload.evidence_summary: Accept based on strong stored description evidence, especially the Steam detailed description. The game explicitly identifies itself as a party fighting game, centers on 2-8 player PvP matches, score-based rounds, 1v1 or free-for-all rules, dynamic hazard-filled arenas, items, and melee combo attacks. That makes the core identity a competitive match-based party brawler rather than a pure traditional fighter; traditional_fighter remains a reasonable secondary because of the light/heavy attacks, combos, and duel option.
